info.title: App Connect Connector Server API
info.description:
HTTP API exposed by an App Connect connector server (called the CRM or adapter server in older code). The configured manifest serverUrl is the HTTPS base URL, without a trailing slash, to which App Connect appends these paths. The contract combines shared @app-connect/core routes with connector-specific routes implemented by this server; fields and supported capabilities can vary by connector where an operation delegates to a connector interface.

Authorization: userJwt is an App Connect CRM-session JWT issued by /apiKeyLogin or /oauth-callback; it is not a RingCentral OAuth token. X-RC-Access-Token carries a RingCentral OAuth credential for RingCentral user or administrator validation. Legacy jwtToken and rcAccessToken query parameters remain active for older clients, but credentials in URLs can leak through logs and browser history and should be avoided.

Legacy response convention: some connector operations return HTTP 200 with successful: false and returnMessage when the request was processed but the CRM operation failed. Callers must inspect the response body rather than treating every 2xx response as business success. Payloads can contain CRM records, communications content, recording URLs, and access tokens; do not log credentials or retain personal data beyond the workflow that requires it.

host.description: Hostname of the deployed App Connect server.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Process liveness and legacy version metadata. Liveness does not imply database, connector, or upstream readiness.

tags.name: Manifest
tags.description: Connector manifests, capability discovery, release notes, and version metadata used by App Connect clients.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: CRM authorization, managed-auth discovery, and App Connect user-session lifecycle.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: RingCentral account settings, managed credentials, server-side logging configuration, and CRM user mapping.

tags.name: User
tags.description: Effective per-user settings and connector-owned CRM user information.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: Connector-backed CRM contact lookup and creation, including synthetic create-new choices.

tags.name: Appointments
tags.description: Connector-backed appointment listing, creation, partial update, refresh, confirmation, cancellation, and status changes.

tags.name: Call Logs
tags.description: CRM call-activity lookup and mutation, temporary note caching, and connector-defined dispositions.

tags.name: Message Logs
tags.description: CRM logging for RingCentral SMS, MMS, fax, voicemail, and grouped conversations.

tags.name: Call Down
tags.description: Server-owned scheduled call-back entries scoped to the current App Connect user.

tags.name: Plugins
tags.description: Account-level plugin installation, advisory licensing, legacy built-ins, and asynchronous completion callbacks.

tags.name: MCP
tags.description: Model Context Protocol metadata, PKCE authorization, JSON-RPC handling, and signed widget-session calls.

tags.name: RingCentral Reports
tags.description: RingCentral account and extension reports generated with stored account OAuth credentials.

tags.name: Google Integrations
tags.description: Bundled legacy Google Sheets and Google Drive flows; these routes are omitted from the public connector reference.

tags.name: Pipedrive
tags.description: Bundled Pipedrive redirect helper and authenticated uninstall callback; these routes are omitted from the public connector reference.

get.description: Returns the literal text OK when the HTTP process can answer. The App Connect client treats any HTTP 200 as reachable and ignores the body. This route does not check the database, connector module, or upstream CRM dependencies.

get.summary: List implemented connector methods for a platform

200.description: Connector-interface availability for the requested platform.

get.description: Returns a boolean capability map for the selected connector. A true value means the connector exports the corresponding handler, not that its upstream service is healthy. App Connect uses these flags to enable features such as name search, call-note caching, dispositions, and user mapping; cacheCallNote also reflects server deployment configuration.

Returns the selected connector manifest used by legacy App Connect clients. If platformName is omitted, the server uses its default connector. Development deployments can override manifest URLs with local server configuration.

parameters.name: platformName
parameters.description: Connector platform key. When omitted, the server returns its default connector manifest.

get.summary: Get release notes for connector versions

200.description: Release notes grouped by server version and connector.

get.description: Returns global and connector release-note sections for versions registered by the selected connector. Global notes are merged only into connector version keys, so global-only versions are not included; the client performs the final presentation merge.

200.description: Version metadata from the default CRM manifest.

get.description: Returns legacy server-version metadata from the default connector manifest. This is a compatibility endpoint and is not a health or update-availability check.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque connector OAuth state. The server and connector use it to restore platform, user, redirect, and anti-forgery context.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: One-time authorization code returned by the CRM OAuth provider.

parameters.name: callbackUri
parameters.description: Full browser callback URI containing the CRM provider result. Legacy clients can send provider fields separately when this value is not encoded.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: Optional tenant-specific CRM hostname retained through the OAuth flow.

parameters.name: tokenUrl
parameters.description: Optional connector-specific OAuth token endpoint retained through the authorization flow.

parameters.name: apiUrl
parameters.description: Optional connector-specific CRM API base URL retained through the authorization flow.

parameters.name: proxyId
parameters.description: Proxy connector identifier retained through the OAuth exchange.

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: Optional RingCentral account context forwarded to connectors that bind CRM OAuth to an account.

parameters.name: rcExtensionId
parameters.description: Optional RingCentral extension context forwarded to connectors that bind CRM OAuth to a user.

parameters.name: userEmail
parameters.description: Optional RingCentral user email forwarded to connectors that need it during OAuth completion.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Optional connector login name used by connectors such as Bullhorn during OAuth completion.

get.description: Completes connector OAuth by passing the provider result and connector context to the selected connector, storing the resulting CRM credentials, and issuing an App Connect CRM-session JWT. Browser flows restore context from state or callbackUri. Connector-specific query fields such as account, extension, email, or username can also be forwarded. MCP authorization stores the completed session server-side and returns plain text instead of a browser token.

post.description: Authenticates an API-key connector and creates an App Connect CRM session. When a RingCentral access token is supplied, the server derives the RingCentral account and extension identities and can combine submitted values with administrator-managed credentials. A successful response returns userJwt as jwtToken; connector rejection is returned as HTTP 400 with an error or returnMessage.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Ignored browser-client compatibility parameter. The server selects the connector from body.platform.

200.description: The connector credentials were accepted and an App Connect user token was issued.

post.description: Invokes the connector disconnect or revocation hook for the CRM user identified by the current App Connect session. It does not delete the App Connect user record or independently invalidate the supplied App Connect JWT.

get.description: Returns the connector-reported license state for the current CRM user. The exact fields are connector-defined. Missing authorization or connector lookup failure can be represented by HTTP 200 with an invalid license state, so callers must inspect the body.

Validates the current user's stored CRM authorization through the connector. HTTP 200 with successful: false means validation ran and the connector rejected or expired the credentials; HTTP 400 represents missing session context, an invalid request, or an execution failure.

parameters.name: connectorId
parameters.description: Private or proxy connector identifier.

parameters.name: isPrivate
parameters.description: Whether connector-scoped managed credentials should be used.

200.description: Managed API-key field state for the RingCentral user and account.

get.description: Derives RingCentral account and extension identity from the supplied RingCentral token and reports whether administrator-managed API-key fields can satisfy the selected connector login. Missing required manual fields and a previous managed-login fallback are returned explicitly. Client-supplied account or extension identifiers are not trusted for this decision.

200.description: Managed OAuth configuration state for the RingCentral account.

get.description: Derives the RingCentral account from the supplied token and returns completed or pending administrator-managed OAuth state for the selected connector. Pending values are returned only to an authorized administrator and can contain sensitive client credentials.

get.summary: Resolve hostname metadata for a platform

500.description: The hostname could not be loaded.

get.description: Returns the CRM hostname stored for the user identified by the App Connect session. The value can be empty for connectors that do not use tenant-specific hosts.

get.summary: Return a hash for the current user info

parameters.name: extensionId
parameters.description: RingCentral extension identifier to hash.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account identifier to hash.

get.description: Produces deterministic server-keyed hashes for supplied RingCentral account or extension identifiers. Either input may be omitted. These opaque correlation values are not authentication proofs and this route does not validate ownership of the identifiers.

Returns account-level connector settings after validating both the App Connect CRM session and the supplied RingCentral administrator token. If the account has no saved settings, the route returns the connector's default empty configuration.

post.description: Validates the supplied RingCentral administrator token and replaces account-level App Connect settings for that RingCentral account. The nested settings and optional custom-connector data come from the request body; this write path does not require a userJwt.

parameters.name: connectorId
parameters.description: Private or proxy connector identifier.

parameters.name: isPrivate
parameters.description: Whether connector-scoped managed credentials should be used.

200.description: Managed authentication field definitions and configured account/user values.

get.description: Returns managed-auth field definitions and decrypted account or extension values for the connector selected by userJwt, after RingCentral administrator validation. Returned credential values are sensitive and must not be logged or exposed outside the administration flow.

200.description: The managed authentication values were updated.

post.description: Stores or removes account-scoped or extension-scoped managed API-key values for the connector selected by userJwt. RingCentral administrator validation is required, and submitted credential values must be treated as secrets.

200.description: The pending managed OAuth values were cached.

post.description: Validates the RingCentral administrator and caches sensitive managed OAuth client values for the account until the connector OAuth flow activates them. Existing pending values can be replaced.

200.description: The pending managed OAuth values were removed.

delete.description: Validates the RingCentral administrator and removes the account's pending, not-yet-activated managed OAuth values. Completed account OAuth configuration is not removed by this route.

200.description: The account managed OAuth values were reset.

delete.description: Validates the RingCentral administrator and removes completed account-scoped managed OAuth configuration for the selected connector. Users may need to authorize again afterward.

post.description: Resolves CRM users for the supplied RingCentral extension list and returns the resulting account mapping. The connector can initialize missing mapping records or indicate that a user session should be revoked. This operation does not save the submitted extension list as configuration.

Clears and rebuilds the account's CRM-to-RingCentral user mapping from the supplied extension list after App Connect session and RingCentral administrator validation. Existing mapping state can be replaced.

get.description: Returns connector-specific additional-field definitions and values used by server-side call logging for the current App Connect CRM user. Despite the admin path prefix, this route does not independently validate RingCentral administrator status.

post.description: Replaces connector-specific additionalFieldValues used by server-side call logging for the current App Connect CRM user. The body must include additionalFieldValues. Despite the admin path prefix, this route does not independently validate RingCentral administrator status.

get.summary: Preload user settings for a platform

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account identifier used when no access token is available.

200.description: Account-level user settings to preload before CRM login.

get.description: Returns account-managed user settings before CRM login. The RingCentral account is derived from a supplied RingCentral token when present, or from rcAccountId for legacy pre-login clients.

post.description: Refreshes connector-owned CRM identity and authorization information for the current App Connect user. A completed refresh that the connector cannot satisfy can return HTTP 200 with successful: false and a returnMessage.

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account id used to resolve account-managed defaults when an access token is unavailable.

200.description: Effective user settings after account-level overrides are applied.

get.description: Returns effective user settings after merging the user's values with account-managed defaults and customization restrictions. rcAccountId is a legacy fallback for resolving account settings when RingCentral token context is unavailable.

200.description: Updated user settings. Legacy connector-hook failures can return an empty object.

empty.summary: Legacy connector hook returned no settings

post.description: Upserts supplied user settings and removes named setting keys for the current CRM user, then invokes the connector update hook when implemented. Connectors using the legacy update hook currently return an empty JSON object after the hook path; other connectors return the effective settings envelope.

parameters.name: overridingFormat
parameters.description: Comma-separated phone-number formats used by CRM-specific matching.

parameters.name: isForceRefreshAccountData
parameters.description: Bypass and refresh cached contact search data.

get.description: Searches CRM contacts by phone number using connector-specific number formats and optional extension semantics. The server can use cached account data unless forceContactRefresh is requested. Results can include a synthetic create-new option marked isNewContact rather than an existing CRM record; clients must not treat that option as a match.

post.description: Creates a CRM contact using the supplied name, phone number, optional CRM entity type, and connector-defined additional submission fields. Connector validation failures can return HTTP 200 with successful: false and returnMessage.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: CRM contact name search text.

get.description: Searches CRM contacts by display name through the connector's optional findContactWithName capability. The returned contact array can include connector-specific fields, and connector-level failure can be represented by successful: false.

200.description: Appointments returned by the active connector.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Ignored browser-client compatibility object. Use startDate and endDate; the current server does not read range.

parameters.name: mineOnly
parameters.description: Ignored browser-client compatibility flag. The current HTTP route does not filter appointments by owner.

parameters.name: forceSync
parameters.description: Ignored browser-client compatibility flag. The current HTTP route does not expose a force-synchronization option.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Inclusive appointment start date. Supply together with endDate.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Inclusive appointment end date. Supply together with startDate.

get.description: Lists CRM appointments through the active connector. Supply both startDate and endDate in YYYY-MM-DD form or neither; when omitted, the connector chooses its default range. The range, mineOnly, and forceSync parameters sent by older browser clients are ignored. Connector-level failures can return HTTP 200 with successful: false.

returnMessage.message: Could not create appointment in Bullhorn.

post.description: Creates a CRM appointment. The wrapped payload form is canonical; direct top-level appointment fields remain accepted for compatibility. Times use ISO 8601 with an explicit offset, and connector-level rejection can return HTTP 200 with successful: false.

patch.description: Partially updates a CRM appointment and preserves omitted fields. The wrapped patch form is canonical. Legacy replacement start-time fields remain supported but must be supplied with their paired duration fields.

post.description: Requests a connector-specific appointment status change. The server normalizes the supplied status to lowercase. Supported status values depend on the connector, and an unsupported change can return successful: false.

200.description: Appointment status update result; connector support varies.

unsupported.summary: Connector does not support status changes

returnMessage.message: This connector does not support appointment status changes.

get.description: Fetches the current CRM representation of an appointment after it may have changed outside App Connect. The response identifies the requested appointment and can report connector-level failure in the body.

post.description: Confirms an appointment through the active connector. Depending on connector support, a successful response can include the current appointment record or only an appointment identifier and status message.
Cancels an appointment through the active connector. Depending on connector support, a successful response can include the affected appointment or only an appointment identifier and status message.

get.description: Checks up to five RingCentral call session IDs for existing local CRM call mappings, scoped by extension identity. When requireDetails is true, matched entries can include connector-provided CRM activity details. A revoked connector session can return HTTP 401.

created.summary: Call log created with a numeric connector id

post.description: Creates a CRM activity for a RingCentral call and records the session-plus-extension mapping used for de-duplication and later updates. Both the browser client and server-side logging service use this route; the latter can create a pending call and later patch it. Synchronous plugins can transform the payload before CRM creation, and asynchronous call plugins are dispatched after the activity is saved.

patch.description: Updates the CRM activity associated with a RingCentral session and the same extension identity used at creation. Only supplied activity fields are changed. Recording download URLs can contain RingCentral access tokens and must not be logged or retained unnecessarily. Registered plugins can augment the update.

post.summary: Cache a call note before full logging
post.description: Caches a call note by RingCentral session ID before full server-side call logging when the connector advertises cacheCallNote. The cache entry expires after one hour. The legacy success response uses a plain-string returnMessage.

put.description: Creates or updates connector-defined disposition values on the CRM activity identified by RingCentral session and extension identity. dispositions is an object keyed by connector-defined field identifier; additionalSubmission is retained only for backward compatibility and is not processed.

post.summary: Create or update an SMS/fax/message log

created.summary: Message logs created with numeric connector ids

post.description: Logs RingCentral SMS, MMS, fax, voicemail, or text records to the CRM. Messages can be written individually or grouped into a shared conversation activity, and previously processed RingCentral message IDs are de-duplicated. Fax and MMS payloads can include a RingCentral access token for attachment downloads; treat it as secret and do not persist it.

200.description: The current user's call-back entries ordered by scheduledAt.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional status filter: called; not called, not_called, or notcalled; or any other value for all entries.

get.description: Lists server-owned scheduled call-back entries for the current App Connect user in ascending scheduledAt order. status=called returns completed entries; not called, not_called, and notcalled return entries whose status is not called; other values return all entries.

post.description: Creates a server-owned call-back entry for the current App Connect user with status scheduled and a generated identifier. contactId, contactType, and scheduledAt are persisted. Current browser clients also send contactName, phoneNumber, and note, but the current data model does not retain those fields.

200.description: The owned call-back entry was updated.

patch.description: Updates allowed fields on a call-back entry owned by the current App Connect user. status, scheduledAt, lastCallAt, contactId, and contactType are persisted. Current browser clients also send contactName, phoneNumber, and note, but the current data model does not retain those fields.

200.description: The owned call-back entry was deleted.

delete.description: Deletes the identified call-back entry only when it belongs to the current App Connect user. A missing or foreign identifier is returned as a bad request with a not-found error.

post.summary: Register a plugin for an account

200.description: The plugin was registered for the account.

post.description: Installs a plugin for a RingCentral account. The route validates administrator access and account identity, resolves the selected platform entry from the Developer Console plugin manifest, calls its registration URL with the short-lived RingCentral administrator token, and stores the returned opaque plugin credential and manifest metadata.

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: Developer Console plugin identifier whose local account registration will be removed.

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account that owns the local plugin registration. It must match the administrator token.

parameters.name: pluginName
parameters.description: Legacy plugin platform key accepted by the route but ignored by the current unregister handler.

delete.description: Removes the account's local App Connect plugin registration after RingCentral administrator validation. The current protocol does not notify the remote plugin server or revoke its credential, so providers need their own stale-account cleanup and retention policy.

get.summary: Get plugin license status for a user

parameters.name: rcAccountId
parameters.description: RingCentral account id whose plugin license is being checked.

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: Plugin identifier from the connector manifest.

get.description: Calls the installed plugin server's optional licenseStatusUrl with the stored account-scoped plugin credential and forwards its advisory status to the browser client. A missing installation returns null; provider lookup failures can be normalized to HTTP 200 with licenseStatus false. Current logging code does not use this result to gate invocation.

post.description: Completes a callback-enabled asynchronous call-plugin task identified by its random task UUID. The UUID is a bearer capability and expires after seven days. A successful report appends note to the existing CRM activity and deletes the task; a plugin-reported failure marks the task failed and is acknowledged with HTTP 200.

404.description: Async plugin task was not found or expired.

500.description: Callback was valid, but App Connect failed to update the CRM log.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone used to group report data.

parameters.name: timeFrom
parameters.description: Inclusive report start time.

parameters.name: timeTo
parameters.description: Exclusive report end time.

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description: Report aggregation interval.

get.description: Generates an account-level RingCentral calls and messages report using OAuth credentials previously stored for the RingCentral account associated with userJwt. The route authenticates an App Connect CRM user but does not independently verify that the caller is currently a RingCentral administrator.

parameters.name: rcExtensionId
parameters.description: RingCentral extension identifier.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone used for report data.

parameters.name: timeFrom
parameters.description: Inclusive report start time.

parameters.name: timeTo
parameters.description: Exclusive report end time.

get.description: Generates RingCentral calls and messages report data for the requested extension using OAuth credentials stored for the App Connect user's RingCentral account. The route does not independently verify current RingCentral administrator status.

Exchanges a RingCentral OAuth authorization code and stores the resulting account-level credentials for report access. The target RingCentral account is taken from the App Connect CRM user session, not from a caller-supplied account identifier.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: One-time authorization code returned by RingCentral OAuth.

200.description: The RingCentral OAuth authorization was completed.

get.description: Creates a presigned URL for uploading a JSON debug report to error-log storage. The URL accepts an HTTP PUT with JSON content and expires after five minutes; it should be treated as a short-lived bearer capability.
Returns the configured OpenAI Apps verification challenge as plain text. This well-known route is public and exists only for deployment ownership verification.
Returns OAuth protected-resource metadata for the MCP endpoint, including the authorization-server location and supported bearer-token method.

get.summary: OAuth authorization server metadata for MCP PKCE clients
get.description: Returns the OAuth authorization-server metadata exposed to MCP PKCE clients, including the authorization, token, and registration endpoints and supported S256 challenge method.

get.summary: Redirect MCP PKCE OAuth authorization to RingCentral

302.description: Redirect to the RingCentral OAuth authorization endpoint.

500.description: The MCP OAuth client is not configured.

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description: OAuth response type.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: MCP public client identifier. When supplied, it must match the configured client.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: OAuth callback URI.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque OAuth state returned to the client.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Space-delimited RingCentral OAuth scopes.

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: PKCE code challenge.

parameters.name: code_challenge_method
parameters.description: PKCE challenge method.

get.description: Validates an MCP OAuth authorization request and redirects it to RingCentral OAuth while preserving the client redirect, state, scope, and S256 PKCE challenge. The optional client_id must match the configured public MCP client.

post.summary: Return MCP public client registration metadata
post.description: Returns metadata for the single configured public MCP OAuth client. This compatibility registration route does not create a new client or issue a client secret.

401.description: An authenticated MCP method requires a RingCentral OAuth token. The response includes WWW-Authenticate metadata.

post.description: Handles stateless Model Context Protocol JSON-RPC requests. Discovery and initialization methods can be public, while tools that access RingCentral or CRM data require a RingCentral OAuth bearer token. Each request establishes its own MCP transport context.

404.description: The requested widget tool is not registered.

post.description: Invokes an allowlisted MCP widget tool with a short-lived signed widget-session token. The token authenticates widget context independently of RingCentral OAuth; unknown or disallowed tools are rejected.

get.description: Serves the bundled Google Sheets picker HTML for the App Connect user identified by the legacy token query parameter. This connector-specific route is omitted from the public API specification.

post.description: Creates a new Google Sheet for the App Connect user and returns its name and URL. Selecting an existing sheet uses /googleSheets/selectedSheet instead.

200.description: The stored sheet association was removed.

delete.description: Disconnects the Google Sheet currently associated with the App Connect user and returns plain text. It does not delete the underlying file from Google Drive.

post.description: Associates an existing Google Sheet and tab selected with a Google access token. The route resolves the Google user from that token before saving the selection.

get.summary: Open admin Google Sheets file picker
get.description: Serves the bundled administrator Google Sheets picker HTML after resolving the App Connect user. RingCentral administrator validation occurs in the subsequent configuration operations.

post.summary: Create or connect an admin Google Sheet
post.description: Creates a new Google Sheet, validates the supplied RingCentral administrator token, and stores the resulting sheet as account-level configuration.

post.summary: Select an admin Google Sheet tab
post.description: Associates an existing Google Sheet with the RingCentral account after Google user lookup and RingCentral administrator validation.

get.description: Returns account-level Google Sheets configuration after validating the App Connect CRM user and RingCentral administrator token.

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: Plugin identifier included in OAuth state.

get.description: Returns the bundled Google Drive plugin's provider authorization URL for the App Connect user. pluginId is preserved in OAuth state for the browser callback.

parameters.name: callbackUri
parameters.description: Original OAuth callback URI containing encoded state.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Google OAuth authorization code.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Granted Google OAuth scopes.

get.description: Completes the bundled Google Drive OAuth flow by reconstructing the provider callback URI, resolving the App Connect user from state, storing Google credentials, and returning the plugin identifier.
Checks whether the bundled Google Drive plugin has stored authorization for the App Connect user identified by the legacy session token.

post.description: Disconnects the bundled Google Drive plugin user identified by the App Connect CRM-session JWT in the body. The token is sensitive and must not be logged.

500.description: The redirect helper page could not be served.

get.description: Serves the static Pipedrive redirect-helper HTML page used by the bundled connector. This route does not complete OAuth or mutate user state by itself.

500.description: The Pipedrive user could not be disconnected.

user_id.description: Pipedrive user identifier whose CRM authorization and App Connect user record will be removed.

schema.description: Pipedrive uninstall payload authenticated with the application HTTP Basic credential.

delete.description: Handles Pipedrive's authenticated uninstall callback. After HTTP Basic authentication, it invokes connector revocation for the supplied Pipedrive user_id and deletes that Pipedrive user record. Repeating the callback for a missing user still returns success.

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: Identifier of a bundled, application-specific plugin supported by this server.

get.description: Returns license and authorization status for a bundled, application-specific plugin selected by pluginId. This legacy route uses userJwt in the query and is omitted from the public connector specification.

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: Identifier of a bundled, application-specific plugin supported by this server.

post.description: Invokes a bundled, application-specific plugin for the current App Connect CRM user. The supported plugin IDs and response shape are hard-coded by this server; this legacy route is omitted from the public connector specification.

userJwt.description: App Connect CRM-session JWT issued by /apiKeyLogin or /oauth-callback. It is not a RingCentral OAuth token. A response can rotate it in X-Refreshed-JWT-Token.

legacyUserJwt.name: jwtToken
legacyUserJwt.description: Legacy query location for the App Connect CRM-session JWT. Prefer Authorization: Bearer. Query credentials can leak through URLs, logs, analytics, and browser history and must never be recorded.

ringCentralAccessToken.name: X-RC-Access-Token
ringCentralAccessToken.description: RingCentral OAuth access token used to validate a RingCentral user or administrator. This credential is separate from userJwt and must not be logged.

legacyRingCentralAccessToken.name: rcAccessToken
legacyRingCentralAccessToken.description: Legacy query location for a RingCentral OAuth access token. Prefer X-RC-Access-Token. Query credentials can leak through URLs and logs; some legacy request bodies also carry rcAccessToken.

mcpBearer.description: RingCentral OAuth access token used by authenticated MCP methods.

widgetSessionToken.name: X-App-Connect-Widget-Token
widgetSessionToken.description: Short-lived signed session token issued to the App Connect MCP widget. It authenticates the allowed widget context without acting as a RingCentral OAuth token.

filePickerJwt.name: token
filePickerJwt.description: Legacy App Connect user-session JWT used by the Google Sheets picker page.

pipedriveBasicAuth.description: Pipedrive application credentials used to authenticate uninstall callbacks.

platform.name: platform
platform.description: Connector platform key from the App Connect manifest.

phoneNumber.name: phoneNumber
phoneNumber.description: Phone number to search, normally E.164.

taskId.name: taskId
taskId.description: Random asynchronous plugin task UUID embedded in the callback URL. Treat it as a bearer capability; tasks expire after seven days.

sessionIds.name: sessionIds
sessionIds.description: Comma-separated RingCentral call session ids. The server processes at most five ids.

extensionNumber.name: extensionNumber
extensionNumber.description: Legacy RingCentral extension number used to disambiguate duplicated call session ids when hashedExtensionId is unavailable.

hashedExtensionId.name: hashedExtensionId
hashedExtensionId.description: Hashed RingCentral extension id used to disambiguate duplicated call session ids. Preferred over extensionNumber when available.

requireDetails.name: requireDetails
requireDetails.description: When true, the server calls the connector getCallLog interface and includes CRM log details.

isExtension.name: isExtension
isExtension.description: Marks the lookup as an internal extension-number lookup.

GenericJson.description: Connector-defined JSON payload. The exact fields depend on the selected connector interface.

ApiKeyLogin.description: Connector and credential data used to create an App Connect user session.

CreateContact.description: CRM contact identity and optional connector-defined creation fields.

CreateCallLog.description: RingCentral call, CRM contact association, note, and optional enrichment data. Recording download URLs and nested tokens are sensitive.

UpdateCallLog.description: RingCentral session identity and the CRM call-activity fields to update.

CallNoteCache.description: RingCentral session ID and note to cache for up to one hour before server-side logging.

CallDisposition.description: Call-log identity and the dispositions selected by the user.

MessageLog.description: RingCentral message conversation, CRM contact association, and optional connector-specific logging fields.

AsyncPluginCallback.description: Completion report for the task identified by the callback URL. successful describes plugin work, not HTTP transport success.

AppointmentStatusUpdate.description: New appointment status. Status support depends on connector capability.

WidgetToolCall.description: Tool name and arguments authorized by the signed widget-session token.

jwtToken.description: Sensitive App Connect CRM-session JWT. It is not a RingCentral OAuth token.

GoogleDriveLogout.description: Legacy bundled Google Drive logout body containing an App Connect CRM-session JWT.

PluginRegister.description: Plugin and RingCentral account identifiers to register.

UserSettingsUpdate.description: User settings to upsert and setting keys to remove.

AdminSettingsUpdate.description: Account-level App Connect settings. Nested setting definitions are extensible.

ManagedAuthUpdate.description: Account-scoped or user-scoped managed authentication values.

AdminManagedOAuthCache.description: Managed OAuth values cached until the account OAuth flow completes.

AppointmentCreate.description: Appointment payload. The wrapper is canonical; the direct form remains for compatibility.

AppointmentPatch.description: Partial appointment update. Omitted fields are preserved.

CallDownCreate.description: Callback scheduling fields. Current clients send display fields that the server accepts but does not currently persist.

CallDownPatch.description: Persisted callback fields to update. At least one handler-supported field is required.

OkJson.description: Successful route-specific JSON response. Consult the operation description for ownership and semantics.

BadRequest.description: Bad request, missing authentication, or connector error.

Forbidden.description: The caller is authenticated but is not permitted to perform the operation.

UnauthorizedSession.description: The user session is expired or revoked.

InternalServerError.description: The server could not complete the operation because of an unexpected internal or upstream error.

messageType.description: Presentation severity. Common values are success, info, warning, danger, and error.

ttl.description: Suggested notification display time in milliseconds.

items.description: Extensible JSON object whose fields are defined by the selected connector or workflow.

details.description: Optional structured details for clients that can present more than the main message.

ReturnMessage.description: Optional user-facing notification returned by connector and App Connect workflows.

platform.description: Connector platform identifier from the App Connect manifest.

apiKey.description: Primary connector credential. It can be omitted when managed authentication supplies the value.

rcAccessToken.description: Legacy body location for a RingCentral access token. Use the X-RC-Access-Token header instead.

rcAccountId.description: Ignored browser-client compatibility field. The server derives the RingCentral account from rcAccessToken.

rcExtensionId.description: Ignored browser-client compatibility field. The server derives the RingCentral extension from rcAccessToken.

userEmail.description: Ignored browser-client compatibility field. Connector credential fields belong in additionalInfo.

hostname.description: Optional connector host name for CRM deployments with tenant-specific endpoints.

connectorId.description: Developer Portal connector identifier used to resolve managed authentication fields.

isPrivate.description: Whether connectorId identifies a private connector.

additionalInfo.description: Connector-defined credential fields, such as tenant or user identifiers.

ApiKeyLoginRequest.description: Credentials and connector context used to establish an App Connect session for an API-key connector.

jwtToken.description: App Connect JWT used to authenticate subsequent user operations.

name.description: Display name returned by the CRM connector.

returnMessage.description: Optional connector message about the completed login.

ApiKeyLoginResponse.description: Successful API-key login result and the new App Connect session token.

error.description: Route-specific error value. It can be text, an Error-like object, or an upstream payload.

successful.description: False when the legacy error envelope reports business failure.

ErrorResponse.description: Error payload. Legacy routes may return a plain string instead.

GenericSuccessResponse.description: Route-specific JSON response. Consult the operation description; this generic shape is retained for legacy endpoints that do not have a stable shared schema.

successful.description: Whether the requested operation completed successfully.

returnMessage.description: Optional status message. Most routes return a structured notification; a few legacy routes return plain text.

BasicMutationResponse.description: Standard result envelope for an App Connect mutation.

id.description: CRM record identifier. The synthetic create-new-contact choice uses the exact sentinel createNewContact.

phone.description: Phone number associated with the matched contact.

phoneNumber.description: Compatibility alias for the matched contact phone number.

type.description: CRM entity type, such as Contact, Lead, or Candidate.

title.description: Contact job title when supplied by the connector.

createdDate.description: Connector-formatted CRM record creation date or timestamp.

mostRecentActivityDate.description: Connector-formatted date or timestamp of the contact's most recent CRM activity.

additionalInfo.description: Connector-defined association, ownership, or display metadata for this contact.

isNewContact.description: Whether this is a synthetic create-new-contact option rather than an existing CRM record.

ContactInfo.description: Connector-normalized CRM contact returned by lookup and create operations.

successful.description: Whether the connector completed the lookup successfully.

contact.description: Matched contacts. The legacy property name is singular even though its value is an array and can include a synthetic create-new choice.

ContactSearchResponse.description: Result of a connector-backed CRM contact search.

phoneNumber.description: Phone number for the new CRM record, normally in E.164 form.

newContactName.description: Display name for the new CRM record.

newContactType.description: Connector-defined CRM entity type, such as Contact, Lead, or Candidate.

additionalSubmission.description: Connector-defined creation or association fields collected by App Connect.

CreateContactRequest.description: Standard CRM contact creation fields plus connector-specific submission data.

successful.description: Whether the CRM contact was created.

ContactMutationResponse.description: Result of creating a CRM contact through the active connector.

phoneNumber.description: External or direct phone number when available.

RcParty.description: RingCentral caller or callee identity embedded in a call record.

type.description: Whether recording was automatic or started on demand.

contentUri.description: RingCentral media endpoint. Access normally requires a RingCentral OAuth token.

link.description: Browser-accessible recording link when RingCentral provides one.

RcCallRecording.description: RingCentral call-recording metadata. Recording and download URLs can be sensitive.

master.description: Whether this is the master leg for the call.

action.description: RingCentral action such as Phone Call or VoIP Call.

result.description: RingCentral leg result such as Accepted, Missed, or Voicemail.

startTime.description: Leg start time in ISO 8601 date-time form.

RcCallLogLeg.description: One RingCentral call leg included in a detailed call record.

accountId.description: RingCentral account identifier associated with the call.

sessionId.description: RingCentral call session identifier used for App Connect de-duplication and CRM mapping.

telephonySessionId.description: RingCentral telephony session identifier when available.

startTime.description: Call start as ISO 8601 date-time or epoch milliseconds. Server-side logging uses epoch milliseconds.

duration.description: Call duration in seconds, or the literal pending for a server-side logging record created before call completion.

durationMs.description: Call duration in milliseconds when supplied by a realtime event.

legs.description: Detailed RingCentral call legs when available.

customSubject.description: Client- or server-generated CRM activity subject.

RcCallLogRecord.description: RingCentral call record accepted from the browser client or server-side logging service. Unknown RingCentral fields are preserved for connector use.

AdditionalSubmission.description: Connector-defined logging fields assembled by the browser client or server-side logging service, including Bullhorn note actions, Clio billing state, and administrator-assignment metadata when configured.

extensionNumber.description: Legacy RingCentral extension-number identity fallback used with sessionId.

hashedExtensionId.description: Deployment-scoped hashed RingCentral extension identifier used by browser logging.

rcExtensionId.description: Raw RingCentral extension identifier sent by server-side logging and hashed by the connector server before persistence.

contactId.description: CRM contact identifier associated with the activity.

contactType.description: Connector-defined CRM entity type for contactId.

contactName.description: Display name of the associated CRM contact.

note.description: User or server-generated note for the CRM activity.

ringSenseTranscript.description: Optional RingSense transcript supplied by server-side logging.

ringSenseSummary.description: Optional RingSense summary supplied by server-side logging.

ringSenseLink.description: Link to the RingSense conversation record.

overridingFormat.description: One or more connector-specific phone-number formats to try. A blank legacy string is also accepted from server-side logging.

CreateCallLogRequest.description: Call, contact, note, identity, and optional RingSense data used to create a CRM call activity.

sessionId.description: RingCentral call session identifier used to find the local CRM activity mapping.

extensionNumber.description: Legacy extension-number identity fallback used with sessionId.

hashedExtensionId.description: Deployment-scoped hashed extension identifier used to find the mapping.

rcExtensionId.description: Raw RingCentral extension identifier sent by server-side logging and hashed locally before lookup.

accountId.description: RingCentral account identifier used for analytics context by server-side logging.

recordingDownloadLink.description: Recording download URL that can embed a RingCentral access token. Treat as secret; do not log or retain unnecessarily.

note.description: Replacement or appended activity note, depending on connector behavior.

startTime.description: Call start as ISO 8601 date-time or epoch milliseconds. Server-side logging uses epoch milliseconds.

ringSenseLink.description: Link to the RingSense conversation record.

telephonySessionId.description: RingCentral telephony session identifier supplied by current browser call updates when available.

UpdateCallLogRequest.description: Fields used to find and partially update an existing CRM call activity.

fullLogResponse.description: Raw connector activity response when details were requested.

CallLogInfo.description: Connector-normalized details for an existing CRM call activity.

matched.description: Whether a local CRM activity mapping exists for the session and extension identity.

logId.description: Connector-assigned CRM activity identifier when matched.

CallLogLookupItem.description: Lookup result for one requested RingCentral call session.

successful.description: Whether lookup completed without connector-session failure.

logs.description: One result per processed RingCentral session identifier.

CallLogLookupResponse.description: Existing-activity lookup results for up to five RingCentral call sessions.

successful.description: Whether the CRM call activity was created or accepted.

CallLogMutationResponse.description: Result of creating a CRM call activity.

successful.description: Whether the connector updated the CRM activity.

updatedNote.description: Final note returned by the connector after update processing.

CallLogUpdateResponse.description: Result of updating an existing CRM call activity.

sessionId.description: RingCentral call session identifier used as the cache key.

note.description: Call note to retain for up to one hour.

CallNoteCacheRequest.description: Temporary call note stored before full server-side call logging.

hashedExtensionId.description: Hashed RingCentral extension id used to find the local call-log mapping.

dispositions.description: Disposition values keyed by connector-defined field identifier.

additionalSubmission.description: Accepted for client compatibility; the current call-disposition handler does not persist or forward these values.

CallDispositionRequest.description: Call identity and connector-defined dispositions to apply to an existing CRM activity.

link.description: Attachment link when supplied by RingCentral.

target.description: Whether this party is the target recipient.

MessageParty.description: Sender or recipient identity on a RingCentral message.

id.description: RingCentral message identifier used for de-duplication.

extensionId.description: RingCentral extension that owns the message.

RcMessage.description: RingCentral SMS, MMS, fax, voicemail, pager, or text record accepted for CRM logging.

MessageCorrespondent.description: CRM association for one external participant in a message conversation.

messages.description: RingCentral messages included in this logging request.

correspondents.description: External participants and their CRM associations.

owner.description: Conversation owner metadata used by supported connectors.

assignee.description: Conversation assignee metadata used by supported connectors.

rcAccessToken.description: RingCentral OAuth access token used only to download fax or MMS attachments. Treat as secret; do not log or persist it.

MessageConversationLog.description: RingCentral messages and CRM correspondents to log individually or as one shared conversation activity.

contactId.description: Primary CRM contact identifier for the message activity.

contactType.description: Connector-defined CRM entity type for contactId.

contactName.description: Display name of the primary CRM contact.

overridingFormat.description: One or more connector-specific phone-number formats to try when resolving message parties.

MessageLogRequest.description: Message conversation, CRM association, and connector-specific fields used to create or update CRM message activities.

successful.description: Whether message logging completed without a connector error.

returnMessage.description: Optional connector status message; null is used when every message was already logged.

items.description: Connector-defined entity identifier, represented as a string or number.

logIds.description: RingCentral message or local conversation-mapping identifiers recorded by this request; an empty list can indicate a no-op.

MessageLogResponse.description: Result of logging an SMS, MMS, fax, voicemail, or message conversation.

successful.description: JSON boolean describing plugin work: true requests a CRM note update; false records task failure.

note.description: Text appended to existing CRM Agent notes when successful is true. Ignored when false.

message.description: Diagnostic result text. Stored as the task failure reason when successful is false; ignored on a successful callback.

AsyncPluginCallbackRequest.description: Plugin completion report for a callback-enabled asynchronous call task.

successful.description: True when the callback report was recorded, including a plugin-reported failure; false for callback validation or processing errors.

message.description: Human-readable validation or processing error when successful is false.

AsyncPluginCallbackResponse.description: App Connect callback-processing result. successful describes whether the callback was accepted or processed, not whether the plugin background work succeeded.

status.description: Requested connector status. The server normalizes this value to lowercase before dispatch.

tool.description: Registered widget tool name. Only explicitly allowlisted tools can be invoked.

toolArgs.description: Arguments accepted by the selected widget tool.

WidgetToolCallRequest.description: Allowlisted MCP tool name and arguments submitted from a signed App Connect widget session.

version.description: Version declared by the default CRM manifest.

ServerVersionInfoResponse.description: Legacy server version metadata retained for older App Connect clients.

getOauthInfo.description: For OAuth connectors, whether getOauthInfo is implemented.

getBasicAuth.description: For API-key connectors, whether getBasicAuth is implemented.

cacheCallNote.description: Whether this App Connect deployment enables the temporary call-note cache.

ImplementedInterfacesResponse.description: Feature flags derived from the selected connector module and server configuration.

extensionId.description: SHA-256 hash of the supplied extension identifier.

accountId.description: SHA-256 hash of the supplied account identifier.

UserInfoHashResponse.description: Server-keyed hashes of RingCentral account and extension identifiers.

presignedUrl.description: Short-lived URL for uploading the debug report.

DebugReportUrlResponse.description: Temporary object-storage upload destination for an App Connect debug report.

pluginId.description: Plugin identifier from the developer portal.

rcAccountId.description: RingCentral account that will receive the plugin.

ownerRcAccountId.description: RingCentral account used to resolve a private or shared plugin manifest.

pluginAccess.description: Manifest lookup mode: public uses the public catalog; private or shared uses ownerRcAccountId; when omitted, the server tries both.

pluginName.description: Plugin platform key used to select the manifest entry when the manifest contains multiple platforms.

rcAccessToken.description: Legacy body location for a RingCentral access token. Use the X-RC-Access-Token header instead.

PluginRegisterRequest.description: Plugin catalog identity and RingCentral account context used for installation.

successful.description: Whether the plugin installation or removal operation completed.

PluginMutationResponse.description: Result of installing or removing an account-level plugin registration.

successful.description: Whether the connector considers the stored CRM authorization valid.

returnMessage.description: Optional connector message explaining the validation result.

AuthValidationResponse.description: Result of validating the current user's stored CRM authorization.

hasManagedAuth.description: Whether the selected connector declares administrator-managed API-key fields.

allRequiredFieldsSatisfied.description: Whether every required login field can be resolved from managed storage; required manual fields make this false.

visibleFieldConsts.description: Required credential field identifiers that the login form should display; null means no visibility override and allows the complete manual form.

missingRequiredFieldConsts.description: Required credential field identifiers that are not currently available from managed storage, including unmanaged fields that must be entered manually.

fallbackToManualAuth.description: Whether a previous managed-auth login failure marked this account and extension to retry with manual credentials.

ManagedAuthStateResponse.description: Readiness of administrator-managed API-key credentials for a RingCentral account and extension.

isAdmin.description: Whether the supplied RingCentral token belongs to an account administrator.

hasAccountOAuth.description: Whether completed account-scoped OAuth configuration exists for the connector.

hasPendingOAuth.description: Whether administrator-supplied OAuth values are cached for an unfinished authorization flow.

oauthValues.description: Completed managed OAuth values that are safe to return to the requesting client.

pendingValues.description: Cached values for the administrator's in-progress OAuth setup. This object can contain sensitive client credentials.

ManagedOAuthStateResponse.description: Account-level managed OAuth setup state for the selected connector.

value.description: Setting value supplied by or returned to the client.

customizable.description: Whether the user may override the account-managed value.

isCustomizable.description: Legacy alias of customizable retained for older clients and connectors.

isRemoved.description: Whether the setting has been removed and should be hidden by clients.

defaultValue.description: Connector-manifest default used when no value is stored.

options.description: Allowed or suggested values for selection-style settings.

UserSetting.description: One connector or App Connect user-setting entry.

UserSettings.description: User-setting entries keyed by their manifest setting identifier.

UserSettingsEnvelope.description: Optional wrapper used by settings preload and update operations.

userSettings.description: Setting entries to create or replace.

settingKeysToRemove.description: Setting identifiers to remove from the user record.

UserSettingsUpdateRequest.description: Per-user settings upsert and removal request.

AdminSuccessMessage.description: Plain-text confirmation returned after an administrator update succeeds.

adminSettings.description: Account-level App Connect configuration, including user-setting overrides and optional custom-connector data.

AdminSettingsUpdateRequest.description: Account-level App Connect settings submitted by a RingCentral administrator.

const.description: Stable field identifier declared in the connector manifest.

required.description: Whether login requires a value for this field.

managed.description: Whether an administrator supplies this field.

managedScope.description: Whether one value is shared by the RingCentral account or stored separately per extension.

ManagedAuthFieldDefinition.description: Connector credential field that supports administrator-managed storage.

hasValue.description: Whether a non-empty value is stored for the field.

value.description: Sensitive decrypted value returned to the authenticated administrator, or an empty string when unset.

StoredFieldValue.description: Administrator view of one stored managed-auth value.

rcExtensionId.description: RingCentral extension that owns these user-scoped values.

rcUserName.description: Display name associated with the RingCentral extension.

fields.description: Stored user-scoped values keyed by connector field identifier.

ManagedAuthAdminUserValue.description: Managed-auth values stored for one RingCentral extension.

hasManagedAuth.description: Whether the connector declares any administrator-managed authentication fields.

orgFields.description: Managed fields whose values are shared across the RingCentral account.

userFields.description: Managed fields whose values are stored separately for each RingCentral extension.

orgValues.description: Account-scoped stored values keyed by connector field identifier.

userValues.description: User-scoped stored values grouped by RingCentral extension.

ManagedAuthAdminResponse.description: Administrator-facing managed-auth definitions and currently stored values.

scope.description: Update values shared by the RingCentral account.

values.description: Field values to create or replace, keyed by field identifier.

fieldsToRemove.description: Field identifiers whose stored values should be deleted.

scope.description: Update values for one RingCentral extension.

rcExtensionId.description: RingCentral extension that owns the values.

rcUserName.description: Optional display name stored with the extension values.

values.description: Field values to create or replace, keyed by field identifier.

fieldsToRemove.description: Field identifiers whose stored values should be deleted.

ManagedAuthUpdateRequest.description: Account-scoped or extension-scoped managed-auth value update.

clientId.description: OAuth client identifier configured for the CRM provider.

clientSecret.description: OAuth client secret. Treat this value as sensitive and never log it.

redirectUri.description: Redirect URI registered with the CRM OAuth application.

scopes.description: Requested OAuth scopes, supplied as one provider-formatted string or a list of scope names.

hostname.description: Optional tenant-specific CRM hostname used by the OAuth flow.

ManagedOAuthValues.description: Administrator-supplied values used to configure account-scoped CRM OAuth.

values.description: OAuth values to hold until the administrator completes or cancels authorization.

AdminManagedOAuthCacheRequest.description: Pending managed OAuth configuration to cache for the RingCentral account.

AppointmentAttendee.description: Normalized attendee returned with an appointment.

AppointmentContactReference.description: CRM contact to associate with the appointment, as an id or typed reference.

AppointmentCreateRequest.description: Canonical wrapped appointment create request or deprecated direct payload.

AppointmentPatchRequest.description: Canonical wrapped appointment patch request or deprecated direct payload.

successful.description: Always true when the connector returned a list.

appointments.description: Appointments in the requested date window.

AppointmentListResponse.description: Appointment list result, including connector-level failures returned with HTTP 200.

HealthResponse.description: Literal liveness response. It does not verify databases, connectors, or upstream services.

type.description: Release-note category such as New or Fix.

ReleaseNoteItem.description: One categorized App Connect or connector release note.

ReleaseNoteSections.description: Release-note lists keyed by global or connector platform name.

ReleaseNotesResponse.description: App Connect and connector release notes grouped by connector-declared server version; global-only versions are not emitted.

startTimeUtc.description: Appointment start timestamp, including its UTC offset.

contacts.description: CRM contacts to associate with or invite to the appointment.

AppointmentCreateInput.description: Fields required to create a connector appointment.

contacts.description: Replacement set of associated CRM contacts.
Replacement set of associated CRM contacts.

startTimeUtc.description: Replacement start timestamp, including its UTC offset.

contacts.description: Replacement set of associated CRM contacts.

startTime.description: Deprecated replacement start time in ISO 8601 form with an explicit offset. The paired durationMinutes field is required.

AppointmentPatchInput.description: Appointment fields to replace; omitted fields remain unchanged.

successful.description: Always false for a rejected appointment operation.

AppointmentFailureResponse.description: Connector-level appointment failure returned with HTTP 200.

successful.description: Always true when the connector created the appointment.

appointmentId.description: Identifier assigned to the created appointment.

appointment.description: Created appointment when the connector returns it.

successful.description: Always true when the connector returned the updated record.

anyOf.description: Successful appointment update or refresh result with the current record.

AppointmentRecordResponse.description: Appointment mutation or refresh result that returns the current record on success.

successful.description: Always true when the connector completed the action.
Always true when the connector completed the action.

returnMessage.description: Connector confirmation when no record is returned.

anyOf.description: Successful confirm or cancel result, with either the current record or a status message.

thirdPartyAppointmentId.description: CRM-native appointment identifier used when opening the record in the CRM.

description.description: Canonical appointment description returned by list operations.

summary.description: Compatibility alias returned by some create, update, and refresh connectors.

participantName.description: Compatibility display name for the primary participant.

startTimeUtc.description: Appointment start timestamp, including its UTC offset.

items.description: Connector-defined entity identifier, represented as a string or number.

attendeeIds.description: Compatibility representation returned by connectors that do not provide attendee objects.

IdentifiedAppointment.description: Appointment record with the identifier required by list and mutation responses.

CallDownEntry.description: Server-owned scheduled call-back entry scoped to one App Connect user.

userId.description: Internal App Connect user identifier. Clients should treat this value as opaque.

contactId.description: CRM contact identifier associated with the callback.

contactType.description: Connector-defined CRM entity type for contactId.

status.description: Call-back state. Current normal values are scheduled and called.

scheduledAt.description: Requested callback time, or null when none was supplied.

lastCallAt.description: Most recent call time after the entry is marked called.

CallDownCreateRequest.description: Fields accepted when scheduling a server-owned call-back entry.

contactId.description: CRM contact identifier to persist with the entry.

contactType.description: Connector-defined CRM entity type. Defaults to contact.

contactName.description: Contact display name sent by the current browser client but not retained by the current persistence model.

phoneNumber.description: Callback phone number sent by the current browser client but not retained by the current persistence model.

note.description: Callback note sent by the current browser client but not retained by the current persistence model.

CallDownPatchRequest.description: Allowed fields for updating a server-owned call-back entry.

status.description: Replacement status, normally scheduled or called.

contactName.description: Replacement contact display name accepted by the handler but not retained by the current persistence model.

phoneNumber.description: Replacement callback phone number accepted by the handler but not retained by the current persistence model.

note.description: Replacement callback note accepted by the handler but not retained by the current persistence model.

CallDownCreateResponse.description: Result of scheduling a call-back entry.

successful.description: Whether the entry was scheduled.

CallDownListResponse.description: Scheduled call-back entries owned by the current App Connect user.

successful.description: Whether the list was loaded.

CallDownMutationResponse.description: Result of updating or deleting a call-back entry.

successful.description: Whether the requested mutation completed.

RefreshedUserJwt.description: Rotated App Connect user-session JWT. Clients should replace the token they sent when this header is present.
